title: 13 Best Herbal Teas For Dry Hair
intro: Herbal teas for dry hair are a natural remedy that refers to the infusion of herbs in hot water to treat and manage dry, brittle, or damaged hair. These teas can treat dry hair by providing essential nutrients, antioxidants, and moisturizing properties that help to lock in moisture, reduce frizz, and promote healthy hair growth. Some examples of herbal teas that can be used to treat dry hair include peppermint tea, which helps to stimulate blood flow to the scalp, rosemary tea, which promotes hair growth and reduces dandruff, chamomile tea, which soothes and calms the scalp, and hibiscus tea, which helps to add shine and softness to dry hair. Additionally, other herbal teas such as lavender tea, green tea, and ginseng tea can also be used to treat dry hair due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to repair and protect the hair.

preparations.herb_desc: Urtica dioica teas have been traditionally used to treat dry hair ailments due to their moisturizing and nourishing properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat dry hair by hydrating the scalp and hair follicles, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing frizz and brittleness. The bioactive constituents of Urtica dioica teas, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, work together to soothe and protect the hair and scalp, reducing inflammation and irritation. Regular use of Urtica dioica teas has been shown to provide long-term benefits, including improved hair elasticity, shine, and overall health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Urtica dioica leaves and flowers, or 2 tablespoons of dried Urtica dioica.
Combine the Urtica dioica with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid into another cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth.
Discard the solids and let the tea cool to room temperature.
Use the cooled tea as a final rinse after shampooing to help moisturize and add shine to dry hair.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Urtica dioica teas can help alleviate dry hair by promoting a healthy scalp and reducing inflammation, but possible side effects include allergic reactions, skin irritation, and digestive issues such as nausea and diarrhea. To use Urtica dioica teas safely, it is recommended to start with small amounts and gradually increase the dose, avoid using it on broken or irritated skin, and be cautious when combining it with other herbal preparations or medications.
preparation_usage: Soak 1 tablespoon of dried Urtica dioica leaves in 1 cup of boiling water.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain it.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a week for 2-3 months.
Massage the tea into your scalp to stimulate blood flow and nourish hair.
Leave the tea on for 15-30 minutes, then rinse your hair with shampoo and conditioner.

preparation_amazon.title: The Republic of Tea — Organic Nettle SuperHerb Tea Tin, 36 Herbal Tea Bags, Naturally Caffeine-Free

preparations.herb_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas, a herbal preparation derived from the leaves of the rosemary plant, have been used to treat dry hair ailments due to their moisturizing and nourishing properties. The essential oils present in this tea, such as camphor and borneol, help to lock in moisture and reduce dryness, thereby alleviating the symptoms of dry hair. Additionally, the antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties of Rosmarinus officinalis teas, including carnosic acid and rosmarinic acid, help to protect the hair follicles from damage and promote healthy hair growth. By incorporating Rosmarinus officinalis teas into one's hair care routine, individuals can benefit from improved hair texture, reduced frizz, and enhanced overall scalp health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Rosmarinus officinalis leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the herbs in boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain the mixture.
Let the tea cool down to room temperature.
Shampoo your hair as usual, then apply the cooled tea as a final rinse.
Leave the tea in your hair for 5-10 minutes, then rinse with cold water.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rosmarinus officinalis teas can be used to treat dry hair, but possible side effects include allergic reactions such as skin irritation, itching, and redness, as well as gastrointestinal issues like nausea and stomach upset. When using these teas to treat dry hair, precautions should be taken to avoid applying the tea directly to the scalp, as it can cause irritation, and to dilute the tea with water before use to prevent overwhelming the scalp with its potent properties.
preparation_usage: Use 1-2 teaspoons of dried Rosmarinus officinalis leaves per cup of water.
Brew a cup of tea and let it cool to room temperature.
Apply the cooled tea as a final rinse after shampooing your hair.
Leave the tea on for 2-3 minutes before rinsing with warm water.
Repeat 1-2 times a week for optimal hair nourishment and hydration.

preparation_amazon.title: Palm Beach Medicinal Herbs - Rosemary Tea - Pure Herbal Tea Series, 30ct

preparations.herb_desc: Aloe barbadensis teas have been traditionally used to treat dry hair, a common condition characterized by brittleness, roughness, and lack of shine. The anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties of this herbal preparation help to soothe and hydrate the scalp, reducing dryness and flakiness. The bioactive constituents, including aloin, aloe-emodin, and vitamins A, C, and E, work synergistically to nourish and protect the hair follicles, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing dryness. Regular consumption of Aloe barbadensis teas can help to lock in moisture, reduce frizz, and add shine to dry and damaged hair, making it a beneficial treatment for this common hair ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water, 2 tablespoons of Aloe barbadensis gel, and a tea strainer.
Cut the Aloe barbadensis leaf and scoop out the gel. Add it to a saucepan.
Heat the water over low heat and pour it over the Aloe gel in the saucepan.
Let it simmer for 10-15 minutes, then strain the mixture through a tea strainer into a cup.
Drink the Aloe barbadensis tea immediately, or store it in the fridge for later use.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Aloe barbadensis teas can be used to treat dry hair by providing essential moisture and nourishment, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, skin irritation, and an increased risk of scalp infections if not used properly. Precautions to take when using Aloe barbadensis teas to treat dry hair include avoiding direct contact with the eyes, using a diluted solution to prevent skin irritation, and performing a patch test before applying it to the hair and scalp.
preparation_usage: Wash your hair with shampoo as you normally do.
Mix 1 tablespoon of Aloe barbadensis tea with 1 cup of water.
Use the tea mixture as a final rinse after shampooing.
Repeat the process once a week for best results.
Leave the tea on for 5 minutes before rinsing with water.

preparation_amazon.title: The Republic of Tea Beautifying Botanicals® Clean Beauty Berry Aloe Herbal Tea Bags (36 count)

preparations.herb_desc: Cymbopogon citratus teas, also known as lemongrass tea, have been traditionally used to treat dry hair, a common condition characterized by brittleness and breakage. The herbal preparation's antifungal and antibacterial properties help to treat dry hair by preventing scalp infections and promoting a healthy scalp environment. The bioactive constituents, including citral and geraniol, exhibit moisturizing and antioxidant properties that help to lock in moisture and protect the hair from damage. By incorporating Cymbopogon citratus teas into one's hair care routine, individuals can experience improved hair texture, reduced frizz, and increased shine, making it a beneficial treatment for dry hair.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Cymbopogon citratus leaves and 2 cups of boiling water.
Steep the leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the leaves.
Add 2 tablespoons of honey to the tea for taste and to help lock in moisture.
Use the tea as a final rinse after shampooing to help nourish and moisturize dry hair.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cymbopogon citratus teas can be beneficial in treating dry hair due to its moisturizing properties, but some individuals may experience side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, or digestive issues when consumed in large quantities or for an extended period. To minimize potential risks, it is essential to start with a small dose, monitor your body's response, and avoid using it for an excessive amount of time, as well as combining it with other medications or herbal preparations.
preparation_usage: Wash your hair with a gentle shampoo as usual
Make a tea by steeping 1 teaspoon of Cymbopogon citratus in 1 cup of boiling water
Let the tea cool down, then apply it as a hair mask
Leave the tea on your hair for 30 minutes to 1 hour, then rinse
Use this tea treatment once or twice a week for optimal results

preparation_amazon.title: J Mac Botanicals, Organic Lemongrass Tea Bags (100 Count), Lemon Grass Tea from Egypt, Manufactured in the USA

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas have been traditionally used to treat dry hair ailments due to their soothing and moisturizing properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat this ailment by providing hydration to the hair follicles, reducing frizz and promoting a healthy scalp. This is attributed to the presence of bioactive constituents such as linalool and linalyl acetate, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to calm irritated scalp and nourish dry hair. Regular consumption of Lavandula angustifolia teas has been reported to provide benefits including improved hair elasticity, reduced dandruff, and a shinier hair texture.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers.
Heat 2 cups of water in a pot until it reaches a boil.
Add 1/4 cup of dried flowers to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and let it cool to room temperature.
Use the tea as a final rinse after shampooing your hair to help lock in moisture and reduce dryness.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can be used to treat dry hair ailments, but possible side effects may include skin irritation, allergic reactions, and an increased risk of interactions with other medications, particularly blood thinners. To use Lavandula angustifolia teas safely, it is recommended to start with small doses, avoid applying it directly to the scalp, and do not use it for an extended period without proper rinse and shampooing to minimize the risk of buildup and over-stimulation.
preparation_usage: Mix 1 teaspoon of Lavandula angustifolia tea powder with 1 cup of water.
Soak hair in tea mixture for 30 minutes to 1 hour.
Shampoo hair as usual after soaking in tea mixture.
Repeat usage 1-2 times a week for better results.
Use 1 cup of tea mixture for short hair, 2 cups for longer hair.

preparation_amazon.title: Tiesta Tea - Lavender Chamomile Herbal Tea | Loose Leaf | Calming Blend with Chamomile and Lavender | Caffeine-Free Herbal | Great for Hot or Iced Brews | Resealable Bulk Pouch, 200 Cups | 8 Ounce

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been traditionally used to treat dry hair ailment due to their moisturizing and nourishing properties. These herbal preparations help to treat dry hair by increasing blood flow to the scalp, improving the health and elasticity of hair follicles, and protecting the hair from damage caused by free radicals. The bioactive constituents of ginkgo biloba teas, including flavonoids, terpenoids, and bilobalide, work together to strengthen hair roots, promote hair growth, and improve the overall texture and appearance of dry hair. The benefits of using ginkgo biloba teas to treat dry hair include improved hair strength, reduced frizz and breakage, and a healthier, shinier hair appearance.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves.
Heat the water in a pot until it boils. Remove from heat.
Add the Ginkgo biloba leaves to the water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture using a tea strainer or cheesecloth into a cup. Discard the leaves.
Drink the tea 2 times a week to help nourish and protect dry hair.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can be a potential treatment for dry hair, but they may cause side effects such as stomach upset, allergic reactions, and increased bleeding risk due to their ability to thin blood. When using Ginkgo biloba teas for dry hair, it is essential to take precautions, such as starting with small amounts and gradually increasing dosage, avoiding consumption before surgery or with other blood-thinning medications, and being cautious when taking with other herbal supplements.
preparation_usage: Drink 1-2 cups of Ginkgo biloba tea daily for 2-3 weeks.
Start with 1/4 teaspoon per cup of tea for first week.
Gradually increase dosage to 1/2 teaspoon per cup of tea.
Use Ginkgo biloba tea 2-3 times a week for maintenance.
Combine Ginkgo biloba tea with a moisturizing hair mask for extra nourishment.

preparation_amazon.title: Tai Chi Think Sharp Energizing Tea (Ginseng Ginkgo Biloba) 12 Bags

preparations.herb_desc: Thymus vulgaris teas, also known as thyme tea, have been traditionally used to treat dry hair ailments due to their moisturizing and nourishing properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat dry hair by providing hydration and promoting a healthy scalp environment, thereby reducing dryness and flakiness. The bioactive constituents of thyme tea, including thymol and carvacrol, contribute to its hair-care benefits, as they possess antifungal and antibacterial properties that help control scalp infections and promote a balanced scalp pH. Regular consumption of thyme tea has been shown to provide benefits such as improved hair texture, reduced dandruff, and a healthier-looking scalp.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Thymus vulgaris leaves or 1 tablespoon of dried leaves.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot and bring it to a boil.
Remove the pot from the heat and add the Thymus vulgaris leaves to the water.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the liquid if using fresh leaves.
Cool the tea and use it as a final rinse after shampooing your hair to help with dry hair issues.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Thymus vulgaris teas can be used as a potential treatment for dry hair, but it may cause side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and an increased risk of interactions with other medications due to its thymol content. When using Thymus vulgaris teas for dry hair, it is essential to take precautions such as starting with small dosages, monitoring your hair and skin for adverse reactions, and avoiding contact with the eyes or mucous membranes.
preparation_usage: Use 1-2 teaspoons of dried Thymus vulgaris in 1 cup of hot water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea.
Shampoo your hair as usual, but use 1/4 cup of the tea as a pre-shampoo treatment.
Apply the tea mixture to your scalp and hair, then shampoo and rinse.
Repeat the process 1-2 times a week for 2-3 months to see results.

preparation_amazon.title: Buddha Teas Organic Thyme Leaf Tea - OU Kosher, USDA Organic, CCOF Organic, 18 Bleach-Free Tea Bags

preparations.herb_desc: Melissa officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat dry hair ailments due to their soothing and moisturizing properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat dry hair by providing hydration and nourishment to the scalp, promoting a healthy scalp environment that allows for optimal hair growth. Bioactive constituents such as rosmarinic acid, luteolin, and apigenin in Melissa officinalis teas help to treat dry hair by acting as antioxidants, reducing inflammation, and promoting collagen production, which ultimately leads to improved hair texture and strength. The benefits of using Melissa officinalis teas to treat dry hair include reduced frizz, improved manageability, and a shinier, healthier appearance of the hair.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Melissa officinalis leaves or 1/2 cup of dried leaves.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot until it boils.
Add the Melissa officinalis leaves to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the leaves.
Apply the cooled tea to your hair as a final rinse after shampooing to help nourish and moisturize dry hair.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Melissa officinalis teas can be used to treat dry hair, but possible side effects may include skin irritation or allergic reactions, as well as interactions with certain medications that may exacerbate dryness or cause other issues. Precautions include using small amounts initially to test tolerance, avoiding use on sensitive skin or scalp, and considering potential interactions with other herbal preparations or supplements.
preparation_usage: Steep 1 tablespoon of Melissa officinalis tea in 1 cup of hot water for 5-7 minutes.
Use cooled tea as a final rinse after shampooing your hair 2-3 times a week.
Leave the tea on your hair for 1-2 minutes before rinsing thoroughly with cold water.
Repeat the tea rinse for 4-6 weeks to see noticeable improvements in hair moisture and shine.
Consume 1-2 cups of Melissa officinalis tea internally daily for added hair nourishment benefits.

preparations.herb_desc: Silybum marianum teas have been traditionally used to treat dry hair, a common condition characterized by dryness, brittleness, and breakage of hair. The herbal preparation's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties help to soothe and protect the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing dryness. The bioactive constituents of Silybum marianum, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and silymarin, have been shown to help repair and protect hair follicles, reducing dryness and brittleness. Regular use of Silybum marianum teas has been reported to provide benefits such as improved hair texture, reduced frizz, and enhanced shine, making it a popular natural remedy for dry hair.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Silybum marianum leaves or 2 tablespoons of dried leaves.
Steep the leaves in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea and stir well.
Drink the tea once a week to help nourish and moisturize dry hair.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Silybum marianum teas can help alleviate dry hair by nourishing and moisturizing it, but it may also cause side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Precautions to take when using this herbal preparation include starting with small amounts, avoiding consumption if you have sensitive stomach or allergies, and being cautious when combining it with other medications or herbal remedies.
preparation_usage: Rinse hair with warm water to open hair cuticles.
Steep 2 tablespoons of Silybum marianum in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain tea and let it cool to room temperature.
Apply cooled tea as a final rinse after shampooing, 1-2 times a week.
Leave tea on hair for 5-10 minutes, then rinse with cold water.

preparations.herb_desc: Equisetum arvense teas have been used as a natural remedy to treat dry hair due to its rich properties that help to nourish and moisturize the scalp. This herbal preparation helps to treat dry hair by providing essential vitamins and minerals, such as iron, calcium, and potassium, that promote healthy hair growth and improve the overall texture of the hair. The bioactive constituents of Equisetum arvense teas, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing dryness and flakiness. Regular consumption of Equisetum arvense teas has been reported to provide numerous benefits for dry hair, including improved hair strength, reduced frizz, and a shinier, healthier appearance.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Equisetum arvense herb and a cup of boiling water.
Place the herb in a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Pour the boiling water over the herb and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into another cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Drink the tea to help nourish and moisturize your dry hair, or use it as a final rinse after shampooing.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Equisetum arvense teas can be beneficial in treating dry hair ailments due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, but possible side effects include digestive issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications. When using Equisetum arvense teas to treat dry hair, it's essential to start with small doses, gradually increase the amount, and monitor your body's response, as excessive consumption can lead to liver damage or other complications.
preparation_usage: Wash your hair with a mild shampoo as usual.
Mix 1 tablespoon of Equisetum arvense tea with 1 cup of water.
Use the tea mixture as a final rinse after shampooing.
Repeat the rinse 2-3 times a week for optimal results.
Leave the tea mixture on your hair for 5-10 minutes each time.

preparations.herb_desc: Saponaria officinalis teas are a natural herbal remedy used to treat dry hair, a common condition characterized by brittleness, breakage, and frizz. The properties of this herbal preparation that help to treat dry hair include its moisturizing and nourishing abilities, which help to lock in moisture and protect the hair from damage. The bioactive constituents of Saponaria officinalis teas, such as saponins and flavonoids, play a crucial role in treating dry hair by providing antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to soothe and calm the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth. The benefits of using Saponaria officinalis teas to treat dry hair include improved hair elasticity, reduced breakage, and a soft, silky texture, making it an excellent natural alternative to commercial hair care products.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Saponaria officinalis flowers and 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the flowers in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a separate container.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey and 1 tablespoon of apple cider vinegar to the tea and mix well.
Cool the tea and use it as a hair rinse for dry hair, applying it from roots to ends and rinsing with cold water.
preparations.preparation_side_effect:
Saponaria officinalis teas can potentially cause skin irritation and allergic reactions when used to treat dry hair, and individuals with sensitive skin may experience redness and itching.

When using Saponaria officinalis teas to treat dry hair, it is essential to start with a small amount and gradually increase the dosage to avoid overwhelming the scalp, and also to rinse the hair thoroughly after application to minimize the risk of dandruff and itchiness.
preparation_usage: Measure 1 tablespoon of Saponaria officinalis dried flowers
Add to 1 cup of warm water to make tea
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes
Use 2-3 times a week, after shampooing for best results
Leave the tea on hair for 15-20 minutes before rinsing

preparations.herb_desc: Cassia auriculata teas have been traditionally used to treat dry hair ailments due to their moisturizing and nourishing properties. The herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids that help to lock in moisture, reduce frizz, and promote healthy hair growth. These bioactive constituents work by forming a protective barrier on the hair shaft, reducing the loss of moisture, and stimulating the scalp to promote hair growth, ultimately leading to softer, smoother, and more manageable hair. The benefits of Cassia auriculata teas in treating dry hair ailments include improved hair texture, reduced dryness, and a decrease in hair breakage, making it a popular natural remedy for hair care.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Cassia auriculata flowers and 2 cups of water in a saucepan.
Heat the water on medium heat and add the Cassia auriculata flowers.
Reduce heat to low and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the flowers. Let it cool down.
Use the tea as a final rinse for your hair after shampooing and conditioning.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cassia auriculata teas can be used to treat dry hair ailments, but it may cause side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and interactions with certain medications. When using Cassia auriculata teas to treat dry hair, it is essential to take precautions, such as starting with a small dose, monitoring your body's response, and avoiding consumption if you have sensitive skin or allergies to ensure safe and effective treatment.
preparation_usage: Measure 1 tablespoon of dried Cassia auriculata flowers.
Steep the flowers in 1 cup of hot water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the flowers.
Use the tea as a final rinse after shampooing, 2-3 times a week.
Leave the tea on your hair for 5-10 minutes before washing.

preparations.herb_desc: Camellia sinensis teas, which are made from the leaves of the Camellia sinensis plant, have been traditionally used to treat dry hair due to their hydrating and nourishing properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat dry hair by locking in moisture, reducing frizz, and adding shine to the hair. The bioactive constituents of Camellia sinensis teas, including antioxidants and flavonoids, help to protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals and environmental stressors. The benefits of using Camellia sinensis teas to treat dry hair include improved hair elasticity, reduced breakage, and a healthy-looking mane.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Camellia sinensis leaves (black, green, or white tea) and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the tea leaves in boiling water for 3-5 minutes. Strain the leaves from the water.
Combine 1 cup of the tea with 1 cup of coconut oil or olive oil in a bowl.
Let the mixture cool down. Then, use it as a hair mask for 30 minutes to an hour before shampooing.
Shampoo your hair as usual and rinse thoroughly. Repeat the process 1-2 times a week for best results.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Camellia sinensis teas can be beneficial in treating dry hair ailments, but possible side effects may include skin irritation, allergic reactions, or digestive issues in some individuals. To use Camellia sinensis teas safely, it is recommended to start with small amounts, avoid using it for extended periods, and monitor your body's response, as excessive consumption or prolonged use may exacerbate dryness or cause other issues.
preparation_usage: Mix 2 tablespoons of Camellia sinensis powder with 1 cup of water.
Shampoo hair as usual, then apply the mixture as a hair mask.
Leave the mixture on for 15-20 minutes, then rinse with warm water.
Use this mixture once a week for 2-3 months for noticeable results.
Combine with other hair care products for enhanced dry hair treatment.

preparation_amazon.title: Ito En Traditional Matcha Green Tea 50 Count Zero Calories, Caffeinated

preparations.herb_desc: Avena sativa teas, derived from the oat plant, have been used to treat dry and damaged hair due to their moisturizing and nourishing properties. The saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids present in Avena sativa teas help to lock in moisture, reduce frizz, and promote healthy hair growth, effectively treating dry hair. The bioactive constituents, including avenanthramides and beta-glucans, in Avena sativa teas work synergistically to repair and protect the hair shaft, leaving it soft, silky, and manageable. Regular consumption of Avena sativa teas has been shown to provide long-lasting benefits, including improved hair texture, reduced breakage, and enhanced shine.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Avena sativa (oat straw), 1 cup of boiling water, and a tea infuser.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried Avena sativa and add it to the tea infuser.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the Avena sativa in the infuser.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then remove the infuser.
Strain the tea and discard the solids. Use the tea as a final rinse after shampooing to help nourish and condition dry hair.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Avena sativa teas can be beneficial in treating dry hair due to its emollient and moisturizing properties; however, possible side effects may include skin irritation, allergic reactions, and increased sensitivity to sunlight. To use Avena sativa teas safely, it is recommended to start with a small amount and gradually increase the dose, avoid using it on broken or sensitive skin, and keep it away from children and pets.
preparation_usage: Use 1-2 tablespoons of Avena sativa tea as a final rinse after shampooing, once a week.
Leave the tea on your hair for 5-10 minutes before rinsing thoroughly with warm water.
Repeat the process once a week for 2-3 months to see noticeable improvements in dry hair.

preparation_amazon.title: 4 oz. Oatstraw Tea Oat Straw Herb Tea Loose Leaf (Avena sativa L.) - 113g Oat Straw Herb Hierba de paja de avena

preparations.herb_desc: Calendula officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat dry hair ailments due to their nourishing and moisturizing properties. The saponins and carotenoids present in this herbal preparation help to lock in moisture, reduce frizz, and improve the overall health of the hair. The bioactive constituents, including triterpenoids and flavonoids, work to repair and protect the hair shaft, reducing dryness and brittleness. Regular use of Calendula officinalis teas can help to restore shine and softness to dry hair, leaving it looking healthy and vibrant.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Calendula officinalis flowers and 2 cups of water.
Heat the water in a saucepan over low heat.
Add the Calendula flowers to the water and let it steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup.
Let the tea cool and use it as a final rinse after shampooing your hair to add moisture and shine.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Calendula officinalis teas can be used to treat dry hair ailments, but it may cause potential side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and increased sensitivity to sunlight, which can lead to photo toxicity. To minimize risks, it is recommended to start with small amounts, patch test on a small area of skin, and avoid direct exposure to sunlight for at least 24 hours after consumption.
preparation_usage: Apply 1-2 tablespoons of Calendula officinalis tea to the ends of your dry hair after shampooing and towel-drying.
Work the tea into a mask, focusing on the damaged or dry areas. Leave it on for 15-30 minutes, then rinse with warm water.
For best results, use Calendula officinalis tea as a once-a-week hair treatment. You can also mix 1/4 cup of the tea with your regular conditioner for added nourishment.

preparation_amazon.title: Organic Positively Botanicals Company, Calendula Herbal Tea Bags, 20 Count

preparations.herb_desc: Ocimum basilicum teas, made from the leaves of the sweet basil plant, have been traditionally used to treat dry hair ailments due to their moisturizing and nourishing properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat dry hair by locking in moisture, reducing frizz, and promoting healthy hair growth. The bioactive constituents present in Ocimum basilicum teas, including eugenol, linalool, and camphor, help to treat dry hair by acting as natural antioxidants, anti-inflammatory agents, and humectants. Regular consumption of Ocimum basilicum teas can provide numerous benefits, including improved hair texture, reduced dandruff, and enhanced shine, making it a popular natural remedy for dry hair ailments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Ocimum basilicum leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the leaves in boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Strain the mixture using a fine-mesh sieve.
Combine 1 tablespoon of honey and 1 tablespoon of apple cider vinegar in the basil tea. Stir well.
Apply the tea as a hair mask. Massage it into your scalp and hair, focusing on the dry areas.
Leave the tea on for 30 minutes to an hour before rinsing your hair with lukewarm water.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ocimum basilicum teas can be beneficial in treating dry hair, but it may cause some side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and stomach upset in some individuals due to its essential oils and active compounds. To use Ocimum basilicum teas for dry hair treatment, take precautions such as performing a patch test before using it extensively, and start with a small amount to gradually increase the dosage, and avoid using it on broken or irritated skin.
preparation_usage: Apply 2 tablespoons of Ocimum basilicum tea to your hair after shampooing, focusing on the ends. Leave it on for 5 minutes.
Rinse your hair thoroughly with warm water and follow with a moisturizing conditioner. Use a cup of the tea as a final rinse to lock in moisture.
Repeat the treatment once a week for 2-3 months to see noticeable improvements in dry hair. Use 1/4 cup of the tea as a deep conditioning mask.

preparations.herb_desc: Taraxacum officinale teas, derived from the leaves and flowers of the dandelion plant, have been traditionally used to treat dry hair due to their moisturizing and nourishing properties. The tea's emollient properties help to hydrate the hair, reduce frizz, and add shine, while its antioxidant properties protect the hair from damage caused by free radicals. Bioactive constituents such as inulin, sesquiterpenes, and flavonoids present in Taraxacum officinale teas contribute to their hair-nourishing effects, promoting healthy hair growth and strengthening hair follicles. Regular consumption of Taraxacum officinale teas can help to alleviate dry hair, restoring its natural moisture balance and leaving it soft, smooth, and manageable.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers, or 1 tablespoon of dried ones.
Heat 1 cup of water in a pot until it's almost boiling.
Add the Taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers to the pot, and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid and discard the Taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers.
Let the tea cool down, then use it as a final rinse after shampooing to help nourish and condition dry hair.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Taraxacum officinale teas can be used as a natural remedy to treat dry hair, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, skin irritation, or digestive issues, such as diarrhea or stomach upset, particularly if consumed in large quantities or for extended periods. Precautions to take when using these teas include monitoring your body's response, starting with small amounts, and avoiding consumption if you have sensitive skin or experience any adverse reactions.
preparation_usage: Wet your dry hair with water and apply 1-2 tablespoons of Taraxacum officinale tea as a pre-shampoo treatment. Leave it on for 15-30 minutes.
Shampoo your hair as usual. Then, rinse your hair with warm water and apply 1 cup of Taraxacum officinale tea as a final rinse. Leave it on for 5-10 minutes.
Rinse your hair thoroughly with cool water and style as usual. Use this treatment 1-2 times a week for best results.

preparations.herb_desc: Aloe vera teas are a natural and effective remedy for treating dry hair, a common ailment caused by damage to the hair cuticle, leading to dryness, brittleness, and breakage. The properties of aloe vera teas, such as its moisturizing and soothing effects, help to treat dry hair by hydrating and nourishing the hair follicles, reducing inflammation, and promoting healthy hair growth. The bioactive constituents of aloe vera teas, including vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like calcium and potassium, help to strengthen the hair shaft, improve the hair's elasticity, and lock in moisture. Drinking aloe vera teas also provides numerous benefits, including improved hair texture, reduced frizz, and enhanced shine, making it a valuable addition to any hair care routine.
preparation_procedure: Get 1 cup of water and boil it in a pot.
Take 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel and add it to the boiling water.
Let the mixture simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid and let it cool down.
Drink the aloe vera tea to help moisturize your dry hair.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Aloe vera teas can help alleviate dry hair by providing essential moisture and nutrients, but some users may experience side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, or even interact with certain medications. To minimize potential issues, it's essential to start with small amounts, gradually increasing the dosage, and be cautious when using Aloe vera teas on sensitive skin or scalp, and avoid using it with other hair or skin treatments that contain similar ingredients.
preparation_usage: After shampooing, apply 1-2 tablespoons of Aloe vera tea to your hair, focusing on the ends.
Leave the tea on for 15-30 minutes before rinsing with cold water. Do this once or twice a week.
For best results, use 1 cup of Aloe vera tea as a final rinse after shampooing and conditioning, once a week.

preparations.herb_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia teas, also known as tea tree oil-infused teas, have been traditionally used to treat dry and damaged hair due to their antifungal and antibacterial properties. The bioactive constituents of tea tree oil, including terpinen-4-ol and cineole, help to repair and restore the hair's natural moisture balance, reducing dryness and brittleness. By promoting a healthy scalp environment and protecting the hair from damage, Melaleuca alternifolia teas help to nourish and moisturize dry hair, leaving it soft, silky, and manageable. The benefits of using Melaleuca alternifolia teas to treat dry hair include reduced frizz, improved shine, and a reduced risk of hair breakage and split ends.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1 tablespoon of Melaleuca alternifolia leaves.
Heat the water in a pot until it starts boiling.
Add 1 tablespoon of Melaleuca alternifolia leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool before using it as a hair treatment for dry hair.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Melaleuca alternifolia teas can be used to treat dry hair, but possible side effects may include skin irritation, allergic reactions, or an increased risk of photosensitivity due to the tea's high concentration of oils. To minimize potential risks, it is essential to use Melaleuca alternifolia teas in moderation, avoid applying the tea directly to the skin, and perform a patch test before using it extensively to treat dry hair.
preparation_usage: Apply 1-2 tablespoons of Melaleuca alternifolia tea to your hair after washing, focusing on the ends.
Leave the tea on for 15-30 minutes to allow it to penetrate and moisturize your hair.
Rinse your hair thoroughly with warm water and style as usual, using a conditioner if needed.

preparations.herb_desc: Thymus serpyllum teas have been traditionally used to treat dry hair ailments, and its properties as a humectant and emollient help to lock in moisture and soothe dry scalp conditions. This herbal preparation helps to treat dry hair by providing hydration, reducing inflammation, and promoting healthy hair growth. The bioactive constituents of Thymus serpyllum teas, including thymol and carvacrol, possess antimicrobial and antioxidant properties that help to combat dryness, itchiness, and flakiness associated with dry hair. By incorporating Thymus serpyllum teas into one's hair care routine, individuals can experience benefits such as improved hair texture, reduced dandruff, and a healthier scalp.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Thymus serpyllum leaves.
Dry the leaves in a low-temperature oven (150°F) for 1 hour.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried Thymus serpyllum leaves.
Steep the dried leaves in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain and let the tea cool before using it as a hair treatment.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Thymus serpyllum teas can be used to treat dry hair, but it may cause side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, or interact with certain medications. To minimize potential risks, it is recommended to start with a small dose, monitor your body's response, and avoid using it if you have sensitive skin or are prone to allergies.
preparation_usage: After shampooing, mix 2 tablespoons of Thymus serpyllum tea with 1 cup of conditioner. Apply the mixture to your hair, focusing on the dry ends.
Leave the mixture on for 15-20 minutes before rinsing it out with warm water. Repeat this process 2-3 times a week for optimal results.
As a final step, apply a small amount of Thymus serpyllum tea as a hair mask once a week, leaving it on for 30 minutes to an hour before rinsing and styling as usual.

preparation_study: According to Current pharmaceutical design, Urtica dioica teas for dry hair may not be specifically mentioned as a treatment for dry hair, but Urtica dioica has been proposed as a potential healing agent for treating hair loss due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
intro_study: According to "Molecules (Basel, Switzerland)", teas for dry hair can be beneficial due to the presence of phytoconstituents and natural ingredients in herbal products that can help improve hair growth, reduce dryness, and promote overall hair health.
